message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:

The user (Yash Singh, SDE at Binocs) wanted to set up a complete personal Claude Code environment (`~/.claude`) including:
- Initialize `~/.claude` as a git repo synced to `https://github.com/yashs33244/my-mac-claude.git` and `https://github.com/yashs33244/claude-god-setup.git`
- Integrate 5 skill collections (anthropic, superpowers, mattpocock, gstack, token-efficient) with flat symlinks for auto-discovery ŌĆö 109 skills total
- Auto-commit + push to both GitHub remotes on every Claude Stop event
- Track ALL files in `~/.claude` (sessions, history, projects, settings, etc.)
- Save full personal profile to global Claude memory
- Add mandatory `/grill-me` + `/plan-eng-review` gates before any new project/feature
- Set up obsidian-second-brain (31 slash commands) with vault at `/Users/tanishqsingh/Desktop/yash desktop files/ybrain`
- Deploy gbrain to Render.com using API key `rnd_rr1YOvQyjlfz1ZW4P0sYLjVxaMMe` with Postgres backend
- After every Claude response completes: summarize conversation and ingest into BOTH the local obsidian vault AND the Render-hosted gbrain

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Claude Code skill discovery: flat `~/.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` required (not nested)
   - Flat symlinks at `~/.claude/skills/` root to expose nested skill collections
   - Claude Code plugin structure: `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` + `skills/`, `agents/`, `commands/` dirs
   - `extraKnownMarketplaces` in settings.json for local marketplace registration
   - Claude Code Stop hook for post-response automation
   - gbrain: personal knowledge brain, CLI + MCP server (stdio or `--http` OAuth 2.1 mode), uses PGLite or Postgres+pgvector
   - gbrain `serve --http --port $PORT` starts an HTTP MCP server with admin dashboard
   - Render API: `POST /v1/postgres`, `POST /v1/services`, `PUT /v1/services/{id}/env-vars`
   - obsidian-second-brain: 31 Claude Code slash commands for Obsidian vault AI management
   - Session transcripts stored at `~/.claude/projects/<sanitized-path>/<session-id>.jsonl`
   - Git auto-commit hook via `settings.json` Stop event
   - Bun runtime required for gbrain (zod/v4 dependency issue with global install ŌĆö works from fresh clone with `bun install`)

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`~/.claude/settings.json`** ŌĆö Core config with Stop hooks:
     ```json
     {
       "effortLevel": "medium",
       "hooks": {
         "Stop": [{
           "hooks": [
             {
               "type": "command",
               "command": "cd /Users/tanishqsingh/.claude && git add -A && if ! git diff --cached --quiet; then git commit -m \"auto: sync claude config $(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M')\" && git push origin main 2>/dev/null && git push claude-god-setup main 2>/dev/null && echo '{\"systemMessage\": \"Changes committed and pushed to claude-god-setup\"}'; else echo '{\"systemMessage\": \"No changes to commit\"}'; fi",
               "timeout": 60,
               "statusMessage": "Syncing ~/.claude to GitHub..."
             },
             {
               "type": "command",
               "command": "[ -f /Users/tanishqsingh/.claude/secrets/.env ] && source /Users/tanishqsingh/.claude/secrets/.env && [ -n \"$GBRAIN_ACCESS_TOKEN\" ] && [ -n \"$GBRAIN_PUBLIC_URL\" ] && ~/.bun/bin/gbrain add \"Session ended $(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M')\" --source claude-sessions 2>/dev/null && echo '{\"systemMessage\": \"Ingested to gbrain\"}' || echo '{\"systemMessage\": \"gbrain not configured yet\"}' 2>/dev/null || true",
               "timeout": 30,
               "statusMessage": "Ingesting to gbrain...",
               "async": true
             }
           ]
         }]
       }
     }
     ```

   - **`~/.claude/.gitignore`** ŌĆö Excludes only `plugins/` (marketplace, auto-updates), `secrets/`, `paste-cache/`, OS files. Everything else (history.jsonl, sessions/, projects/, settings.json, backups/, statsig/, etc.) IS tracked.

   - **`~/.claude/CLAUDE.md`** ŌĆö Updated with mandatory workflow gates, Yash's identity, updated skills collections (anthropic, superpowers, mattpocock, gstack, token-efficient, obsidian-second-brain), setup URL now `claude-god-setup`, remote info.

   - **`~/.claude/secrets/.env`** (gitignored, chmod 600):
     ```
     RENDER_API_KEY=rnd_rr1YOvQyjlfz1ZW4P0sYLjVxaMMe
     GBRAIN_PUBLIC_URL=  # to fill: https://gbrain-757w.onrender.com
     GBRAIN_ACCESS_TOKEN=  # to fill after deploy
     GBRAIN_DATABASE_URL=  # external DB URL
     ```

   - **`~/.claude/gbrain/render.yaml`**:
     ```yaml
     services:
       - type: web
         name: gbrain
         runtime: node
         buildCommand: npm install -g bun && bun install --frozen-lockfile
         startCommand: bun run src/cli.ts serve --http --port $PORT --public-url $RENDER_EXTERNAL_URL
         healthCheckPath: /health
         envVars:
           - key: DATABASE_URL
             fromDatabase: {name: gbrain-db, property: connectionString}
           - key: GBRAIN_ENGINE
             value: postgres
           - key: NODE_ENV
             value: production
     databases:
       - name: gbrain-db
         plan: free
         postgresMajorVersion: 16
     ```

   - **`~/.claude/projects/-Users-tanishqsingh--claude/memory/user_yash_profile.md`** ŌĆö Full Yash profile (experience at Binocs/ViewR/IIT Mandi, projects FinalCV/Chess, full stack, links, achievements).

   - **`~/.claude/projects/-Users-tanishqsingh--claude/memory/feedback_workflow_rules.md`** ŌĆö Rule: always run `/grill-me` + `/plan-eng-review` before new projects/features.

   - **`~/.claude/projects/-Users-tanishqsingh--claude/memory/MEMORY.md`** ŌĆö Index file pointing to both memory files.

   - **`~/.claude/skills/token-efficient/token-efficient-coding/SKILL.md`**, `token-efficient-analysis/SKILL.md`, `token-efficient-rules/SKILL.md` ŌĆö Created from drona23/claude-token-efficient profiles (no SKILL.md existed upstream).

   - **`~/.claude/.claude-plugin/plugin.json`** ŌĆö Updated to v2.0.0 with all 5 collections + 109 skills listed.

   - **`~/.claude/.claude-plugin/marketplace.json`** ŌĆö Updated with gstack (52 skills) and token-efficient (3 skills) plugin groups.

   - **`~/.claude/commands/`** ŌĆö 31 obsidian-second-brain commands installed (`obsidian-*.md`, `research.md`, `x-read.md`, etc.)

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **Embedded git repos**: obsidian-second-brain and gbrain copied with their `.git` dirs ŌåÆ git warned "adding embedded git repository" ŌåÆ fixed by removing `.git` dirs with `rm -rf skills/obsidian-second-brain/.git` and `rm -rf gbrain/.git`, then re-adding
   - **gbrain global install zod/v4 error**: `bun install -g github:garrytan/gbrain` failed with "Cannot find module 'zod/v4'" ŌåÆ fixed by cloning fresh to `/tmp/gbrain-fresh` and running `bun install` locally (works at v0.27.0)
   - **gbrain install native modules**: First install failed on `mongodb-client-encryption` gyp build ŌåÆ fixed with `--ignore-scripts` flag (partial workaround; full local install needed)
   - **Render API `missing environment variable value`**: First attempt used wrong envVars format with `fromDatabase` inline ŌåÆ fixed by using `envSpecificDetails` wrapper and setting DATABASE_URL via separate `PUT /v1/services/{id}/env-vars` call
   - **Render API `envSpecificDetails` required**: Second attempt missing `envSpecificDetails` wrapper for non-docker services ŌåÆ fixed by nesting build/start commands inside `envSpecificDetails`
   - **Skills nested too deep**: Skills at `skills/anthropic/algorithmic-art/SKILL.md` not discovered by Claude ŌåÆ fixed by creating flat symlinks at `~/.claude/skills/<name>` pointing to the actual directories
   - **`gstack/` gitignored**: Old `.gitignore` had `gstack/` rule ŌåÆ removed it, added `!/skills/gstack` exception
   - **`qa` name conflict**: Both gstack and mattpocock/deprecated have `qa` skill ŌåÆ linked gstack as `gstack-qa`; `review` ŌåÆ `gstack-review`; root SKILL.md ŌåÆ `gstack-main`

5. Problem Solving:
   - **Skill auto-discovery**: Solved by creating flat symlinks at `~/.claude/skills/` root so Claude finds `SKILL.md` one level deep
   - **Plugin manifest format**: The `skills` array in plugin.json uses relative paths from the plugin.json location, not the repo root ŌĆö updated paths accordingly
   - **gbrain on Render**: Successfully created Render Postgres DB (`gbrain-db`, ID `dpg-d7spk2sm0tmc73ddk0fg-a`) and web service (`gbrain`, ID `srv-d7spkcv7f7vs739go7kg`, URL `https://gbrain-757w.onrender.com`) deploying from `yashs33244/claude-god-setup` repo, `gbrain/` subdirectory
   - **Ingestion hook**: Currently a no-op placeholder until gbrain is deployed and `GBRAIN_ACCESS_TOKEN` + `GBRAIN_PUBLIC_URL` are filled in secrets/.env

6. All User Messages:
   - "https://github.com/yashs33244/my-mac-claude.git setup thi"
   - "cannt get all the skills and agents that are in ghttps://github.com/yashs33244/my-mac-claude/tree/main" (with /reload-plugins showing 0 plugins 0 skills)
   - "setup this also https://github.com/garrytan/gstack.git, https://github.com/drona23/claude-token-efficient.git, https://github.com/mattpocock/skills, https://github.com/anthropics/skills, and make sure to push final claude changes to this my setup https://github.com/yashs33244/my-mac-claude.git and after setting up this make sure i am able to access every skill with their slash commands"
   - "make sure my ~/.claude repo is in sync with echo "# claude-god-setup" >> README.md [git init commands]... make sure after every query it commit and pushed the changes here" (pointing to claude-god-setup)
   - "not only the skills folder all the things like session and all the things all of the things in ~/.claude folder"
   - [Long message with full Yash profile, links, experience, projects, stack, achievements] + "before starting any new project or implementing a new feature I claude will /grill-me and /plan-eng-review also after this setup https://github.com/eugeniughelbur/obsidian-second-brain.git (for local obsidian local brain) this https://github.com/garrytan/gbrain.git (this is to be done in render (add hooks every information should be uploaded and ingested)) here is render.com api key rnd_rr1YOvQyjlfz1ZW4P0sYLjVxaMMe"
   - "use the folder '/Users/tanishqsingh/Desktop/yash desktop files/ybrain' for local brain The second brain should have a hook on this. And G-BRAIN also. Whenever my Claude response is completed, whenever I give a prompt and the response is completed, the information in the chat and all the things, the summary type of thing, should be ingested in the brain. It should be like post-processing for that. To deploy on Render, you have all the things. Just deploy it."

7. Pending Tasks:
   - **Complete gbrain Render deployment**: Service is deploying at `https://gbrain-757w.onrender.com` but needs:
     - Verify the build succeeds (rootDir issue ŌĆö `gbrain/` subdir may not be picked up correctly, may need to update)
     - Run `gbrain auth create` once deployed to get an access token
     - Fill `GBRAIN_PUBLIC_URL` and `GBRAIN_ACCESS_TOKEN` in `~/.claude/secrets/.env`
   - **Initialize local gbrain** with vault at `/Users/tanishqsingh/Desktop/yash desktop files/ybrain`:
     - Run `gbrain init` pointing to that directory
     - Configure PGLite or connect to Render Postgres externally
   - **Build the actual ingestion hook script**: Current Stop hook only does a trivial `gbrain add "Session ended..."` ŌĆö needs to:
     - Read the actual session transcript from `~/.claude/projects/.../session.jsonl`
     - Summarize it (using Claude API or extracting key info)
     - Write a markdown note to the obsidian vault at `/Users/tanishqsingh/Desktop/yash desktop files/ybrain`
     - Ingest the note into both local gbrain (PGLite) AND remote gbrain on Render
   - **obsidian vault initialization**: Run `/obsidian-init` to generate `_CLAUDE.md` for the vault at `/Users/tanishqsingh/Desktop/yash desktop files/ybrain`
   - **Commit and push** the completed Render deployment details and final hook script to both remotes

8. Current Work:
   Immediately before this summary, was in the middle of deploying gbrain to Render.com. Completed steps:
   - Created Render Postgres DB: `gbrain-db` (ID: `dpg-d7spk2sm0tmc73ddk0fg-a`)
     - External: `postgresql://gbrain_db_user:DioEKOt2zKg3TQS5dvsPy6CCZ8tQ3d6d@dpg-d7spk2sm0tmc73ddk0fg-a.oregon-postgres.render.com:5432/gbrain_db`
     - Internal: `postgresql://gbrain_db_user:DioEKOt2zKg3TQS5dvsPy6CCZ8tQ3d6d@dpg-d7spk2sm0tmc73ddk0fg-a/gbrain_db`
   - Created gbrain web service (ID: `srv-d7spkcv7f7vs739go7kg`, URL: `https://gbrain-757w.onrender.com`)
     - Repo: `https://github.com/yashs33244/claude-god-setup`, branch `main`, rootDir `gbrain`
     - Build: `npm install -g bun && bun install --frozen-lockfile`
     - Start: `bun run src/cli.ts serve --http --port $PORT --public-url $RENDER_EXTERNAL_URL`
   - Set env vars on service: DATABASE_URL (internal connection), GBRAIN_ENGINE=postgres, NODE_ENV=production, PORT=10000
   - The last API call (`PUT /v1/services/srv-d7spkcv7f7vs739go7kg/env-vars`) returned success showing DATABASE_URL was set

9. Optional Next Step:
   The user's most recent explicit request was: "use the folder '/Users/tanishqsingh/Desktop/yash desktop files/ybrain' for local brain The second brain should have a hook on this. And G-BRAIN also. Whenever my Claude response is completed, whenever I give a prompt and the response is completed, the information in the chat and all the things, the summary type of thing, should be ingested in the brain... To deploy on Render, you have all the things. Just deploy it."

   2. **Build the ingestion script** at `~/.claude/scripts/ingest-session.sh` that: reads `~/.claude/projects/-Users-tanishqsingh--claude/<session-id>.jsonl`, creates a structured markdown note, writes it to `/Users/tanishqsingh/Desktop/yash desktop files/ybrain/claude-sessions/`, and calls `gbrain add` to ingest it

file.content:
services:
  # gbrain HTTP MCP server with OAuth + admin dashboard
  - type: web
    name: gbrain
    runtime: node
    buildCommand: npm install -g bun && bun install --frozen-lockfile
    startCommand: bun run src/cli.ts serve --http --port $PORT --public-url $RENDER_EXTERNAL_URL
    healthCheckPath: /health
    envVars:
      - key: DATABASE_URL
        fromDatabase:
          name: gbrain-db
          property: connectionString
      - key: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
        sync: false
      - key: OPENAI_API_KEY
        sync: false
      - key: GBRAIN_ENGINE
        value: postgres
      - key: NODE_ENV
        value: production

databases:
  # Render-managed Postgres with pgvector
  - name: gbrain-db
    plan: free
    postgresMajorVersion: 16

# Source repo internals (keep out of combined repo)
/skills/.git
/skills/.claude-plugin
/skills/.gitignore
/skills/README.md
/skills/THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md
/skills/spec
/skills/template
/skills/skills

# Old top-level source dirs (no longer needed)
/superpowers/
matt-skill/

# Marketplace plugin cache ŌĆö very large and auto-updated by Claude Code on every startup.
# The plugin structure is tracked in .claude-plugin/ instead.
plugins/

# Secrets ŌĆö NEVER commit
secrets/

# Truly temporary / ephemeral files
paste-cache/
*.swp
*.swo

# OS files
.DS_Store

# Node
node_modules/
package-lock.json
yarn.lock

# CLAUDE.md

GBrain is a personal knowledge brain and GStack mod for agent platforms. Pluggable
engines: PGLite (embedded Postgres via WASM, zero-config default) or Postgres + pgvector
+ hybrid search in a managed Supabase instance. `gbrain init` defaults to PGLite;
suggests Supabase for 1000+ files. GStack teaches agents how to code. GBrain teaches
agents everything else: brain ops, signal detection, content ingestion, enrichment,
cron scheduling, reports, identity, and access control.

## Two organizational axes (read this first)

GBrain knowledge is organized along two orthogonal axes. Users AND agents must
understand both, or queries misroute silently.

- **Brain** ŌĆö WHICH DATABASE. Your personal brain is `host`. You can mount
  additional brains (team-published, each with their own DB and access policy)
  via `gbrain mounts add` (v0.19+). Routing: `--brain`, `GBRAIN_BRAIN_ID`,
  `.gbrain-mount` dotfile.
- **Source** ŌĆö WHICH REPO INSIDE THE DATABASE. A brain can hold many sources
  (wiki, gstack, openclaw, essays). Slugs scope per source. Routing:
  `--source`, `GBRAIN_SOURCE`, `.gbrain-source` dotfile.

Both axes follow the same 6-tier resolution pattern. Read
`docs/architecture/brains-and-sources.md` for topology diagrams (personal, team
mount, CEO-class with multiple team brains) and
`skills/conventions/brain-routing.md` for the agent-facing decision table.

## Architecture

Contract-first: `src/core/operations.ts` defines ~41 shared operations (adds `find_orphans` in v0.12.3). CLI and MCP
server are both generated from this single source. Engine factory (`src/core/engine-factory.ts`)
dynamically imports the configured engine (`'pglite'` or `'postgres'`). Skills are fat
markdown files (tool-agnostic, work with both CLI and plugin contexts).

**Trust boundary:** `OperationContext.remote` distinguishes trusted local CLI callers
(`remote: false` set by `src/cli.ts`) from untrusted agent-facing callers
(`remote: true` set by `src/mcp/server.ts`). Security-sensitive operations like
`file_upload` tighten filesystem confinement when `remote=true` and default to
strict behavior when unset.

## Key files

- `src/core/operations.ts` ŌĆö Contract-first operation definitions (the foundation). Also exports upload validators: `validateUploadPath`, `validatePageSlug`, `validateFilename`, plus `matchesSlugAllowList(slug, prefixes)` (v0.23 glob matcher: `<prefix>/*` matches recursive children; bare `<prefix>` matches exact only). `OperationContext.remote` flags untrusted callers; `OperationContext.allowedSlugPrefixes` (v0.23) is the trusted-workspace allow-list set by the dream cycle. `put_page` enforces: when `viaSubagent` and `allowedSlugPrefixes` is set, slug must match the allow-list; else the legacy `wiki/agents/<id>/...` namespace check applies. Auto-link enabled for trusted-workspace writes (skipped only when `remote=true && !trustedWorkspace`). As of v0.26.0, every `Operation` also carries `scope?: 'read' | 'write' | 'admin'` + `localOnly?: boolean`. All ops are annotated; `sync_brain`, `file_upload`, `file_list`, and `file_url` are `admin + localOnly` (rejected over HTTP). `OperationContext.auth?: AuthInfo` is threaded through HTTP dispatch for scope enforcement in `serve-http.ts` before the op runs. **v0.26.9 (D12 + F7b):** `OperationContext.remote` is now a REQUIRED field in the TypeScript type ŌĆö the compiler is the first defense against transports that forget to set it. Four trust-boundary call sites (`put_page` allowlist, file_upload trust-narrowing, submit_job protected-name guard, auto-link skip) flipped from falsy-default (`!ctx.remote`) to fail-closed semantics (`ctx.remote === false` for "trusted-only" sites and `ctx.remote !== false` for "untrust unless explicit-false"). Anything that isn't strictly `false` is now treated as remote. Closed an HTTP MCP shell-job RCE: a `read+write`-scoped OAuth token could submit `shell` jobs because the HTTP request handler's literal context skipped `remote: true` and `submit_job`'s protected-name guard saw a falsy undefined. Stdio MCP set the field correctly via dispatch.ts; HTTP inlined a parallel context-builder for several releases and lost it.
- `src/core/engine.ts` ŌĆö Pluggable engine interface (BrainEngine). `clampSearchLimit(limit, default, cap)` takes an explicit cap so per-operation caps can be tighter than `MAX_SEARCH_LIMIT`. Exports `LinkBatchInput` / `TimelineBatchInput` for the v0.12.1 bulk-insert API (`addLinksBatch` / `addTimelineEntriesBatch`). As of v0.13.1, `BrainEngine` has a `readonly kind: 'postgres' | 'pglite'` discriminator so migrations (`src/core/migrate.ts`) and other consumers can branch on engine without `instanceof` + dynamic imports.
- `src/core/engine-factory.ts` ŌĆö Engine factory with dynamic imports (`'pglite'` | `'postgres'`)
- `src/core/pglite-engine.ts` ŌĆö PGLite (embedded Postgres 17.5 via WASM) implementation, all 40 BrainEngine methods. `addLinksBatch` / `addTimelineEntriesBatch` use multi-row `unnest()` with manual `$N` placeholders. As of v0.13.1, `connect()` wraps `PGlite.create()` in a try/catch that emits an actionable error naming the macOS 26.3 WASM bug (#223) and pointing at `gbrain doctor`; the lock is released on failure so the next process can retry cleanly. v0.22.0: `searchKeyword` and `searchKeywordChunks` multiply `ts_rank` by the source-factor CASE expression at the chunk-grain level; `searchVector` becomes a two-stage CTE ŌĆö inner CTE keeps `ORDER BY cc.embedding <=> vec` so HNSW stays usable, outer SELECT re-ranks by `raw_score * source_factor`. Inner LIMIT scales with offset to preserve pagination contract. As of v0.22.6.1, `initSchema()` calls `applyForwardReferenceBootstrap()` BEFORE replaying SCHEMA_SQL ŌĆö probes for the specific forward-referenced state the embedded schema blob needs (`pages.source_id`, `links.link_source`, `links.origin_page_id`, `content_chunks.symbol_name`, `content_chunks.language`, `sources` FK target table) and adds only what's missing. Closes the upgrade-wedge bug class that bit users 10+ times across 6 schema versions over 2 years (#239/#243/#266/#357/#366/#374/#375/#378/#395/#396). No-op on fresh installs and modern brains.
- `src/core/pglite-schema.ts` ŌĆö PGLite-specific DDL (pgvector, pg_trgm, triggers)
- `src/core/postgres-engine.ts` ŌĆö Postgres + pgvector implementation (Supabase / self-hosted). `addLinksBatch` / `addTimelineEntriesBatch` use `INSERT ... SELECT FROM unnest($1::text[], ...) JOIN pages ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING RETURNING 1` ŌĆö 4-5 array params regardless of batch size, sidesteps the 65535-parameter cap. As of v0.12.3, `searchKeyword` / `searchVector` scope `statement_timeout` via `sql.begin` + `SET LOCAL` so the GUC dies with the transaction instead of leaking across the pooled postgres.js connection (contributed by @garagon). `getEmbeddingsByChunkIds` uses `tryParseEmbedding` so one corrupt row skips+warns instead of killing the query. v0.22.0: `searchKeyword`, `searchKeywordChunks`, and `searchVector` apply source-aware ranking by inlining the source-factor CASE and `NOT (col LIKE ŌĆ”)` hard-exclude clause from `src/core/search/sql-ranking.ts`. `searchVector` switches to a two-stage CTE (HNSW-safe inner ORDER BY, source-boost re-rank in the outer SELECT) and carries `p.source_id` through innerŌåÆouter for v0.18 multi-source callers. v0.22.1 (#406): `_savedConfig` retains the connect config; `reconnect()` tears down + recreates the pool from saved config (called by supervisor watchdog after 3 consecutive health-check failures). `executeRaw` is a single-statement passthrough ŌĆö no per-call retry (D3 dropped that as unsound for non-idempotent statements; recovery is supervisor-driven). v0.22.1 (#363, contributed by @orendi84): `connect()` applies `resolveSessionTimeouts()` from `db.ts` as connection-time startup parameters (`statement_timeout`, `idle_in_transaction_session_timeout`) so orphan pgbouncer backends can't hold locks for hours. v0.22.1 (#409, contributed by @atrevino47): `countStaleChunks()` + `listStaleChunks()` server-side-filter on `embedding IS NULL` for `embed --stale`, eliminating ~76 MB/call client-side pull on a fully-embedded brain; `upsertChunks()` resets both `embedding` AND `embedded_at` to NULL when chunk_text changes without a new embedding (consistency). As of v0.22.6.1, `initSchema()` calls `applyForwardReferenceBootstrap()` BEFORE replaying SCHEMA_SQL on the same forward-reference probe set as the PGLite engine, so old Postgres brains pinned at v0.13/v0.18/v0.19 walk forward cleanly instead of wedging on `column "..." does not exist`.
- `src/core/utils.ts` ŌĆö Shared SQL utilities extracted from postgres-engine.ts. Exports `parseEmbedding(value)` (throws on unknown input, used by migration + ingest paths where data integrity matters) and as of v0.12.3 `tryParseEmbedding(value)` (returns `null` + warns once per process, used by search/rescore paths where availability matters more than strictness). **v0.26.9 (D14):** adds `isUndefinedColumnError(err)` predicate ŌĆö pattern-matches Postgres SQLSTATE 42703 / "column ... does not exist" with engine-driver shape variation tolerated. Replaces bare `catch {}` blocks in `oauth-provider.ts` so genuine errors (lock timeout, network blip, permission denied) propagate while column-missing falls through to the legacy fallback path. Reusable from any future code that needs the same column-existence probe semantics.
- `src/core/db.ts` ŌĆö Connection management, schema initialization. v0.22.1 (#363, contributed by @orendi84): `resolveSessionTimeouts()` returns `statement_timeout` + `idle_in_transaction_session_timeout` (defaults: 5min each, env-overridable via `GBRAIN_STATEMENT_TIMEOUT` / `GBRAIN_IDLE_TX_TIMEOUT` / `GBRAIN_CLIENT_CHECK_INTERVAL`). Both `connect()` (module singleton) and `PostgresEngine.connect()` (worker pool) consume the result via postgres.js's `connection` option, sending GUCs as startup parameters that survive PgBouncer transaction mode (unlike the prior `setSessionDefaults` post-pool SET, kept as a back-compat no-op shim).
- `src/commands/migrate-engine.ts` ŌĆö Bidirectional engine migration (`gbrain migrate --to supabase/pglite`)
- `src/core/import-file.ts` ŌĆö importFromFile + importFromContent (chunk + embed + tags)
- `src/core/sync.ts` ŌĆö Pure sync functions (manifest parsing, filtering, slug conversion). v0.22.12 (#500, foundation by @wintermute via #501): `classifyErrorCode(errorMsg)` regex-based classifier with 12 codes (`SLUG_MISMATCH`, `YAML_PARSE`, `YAML_DUPLICATE_KEY`, `MISSING_OPEN`, `MISSING_CLOSE`, `NESTED_QUOTES`, `EMPTY_FRONTMATTER`, `NULL_BYTES`, `INVALID_UTF8`, `STATEMENT_TIMEOUT`, `FILE_TOO_LARGE`, `SYMLINK_NOT_ALLOWED`) plus `UNKNOWN` fallback. `summarizeFailuresByCode(failures)` returns sorted `[{code, count}]`. `code?` optional field on `SyncFailure`; backfilled at ack time on pre-v0.22.12 entries. `acknowledgeSyncFailures()` returns `AcknowledgeResult { count, summary }`. Three regexes (`MISSING_OPEN`, `MISSING_CLOSE`, `EMPTY_FRONTMATTER`) broadened to match actual `markdown.ts:159-244` validator message strings, not just the literal code-name prefix. `FILE_TOO_LARGE` covers all three production size sites in `import-file.ts:199, 352, 401`; `SYMLINK_NOT_ALLOWED` covers the rejection at `:347`. Closes the silent-skip pattern that motivated #500.
- `src/core/storage.ts` ŌĆö Pluggable storage interface (S3, Supabase Storage, local)
- `src/core/storage-config.ts` (v0.22.11) ŌĆö Storage tiering: `loadStorageConfig` reads `gbrain.yml`, normalizes deprecated keys (`git_tracked` / `supabase_only`) to canonical (`db_tracked` / `db_only`) with once-per-process deprecation warning, and runs `normalizeAndValidateStorageConfig` (auto-fixes missing trailing `/`, throws `StorageConfigError` on tier overlap). Path-segment matcher: `media/x/` does NOT match `media/xerox/foo`. Replaces gray-matter (broken on delimiter-less YAML) with a dedicated parser for the `gbrain.yml` shape.
- `src/core/disk-walk.ts` (v0.22.11) ŌĆö `walkBrainRepo(repoPath)` returns `Map<slug, {size, mtimeMs}>` from one recursive `readdirSync`. Skips dot-dirs, `node_modules`, non-`.md` files. Used by `gbrain storage status` to replace per-page `existsSync + statSync` (~400K syscalls on 200K-page brains ŌåÆ tens).
- `src/commands/storage.ts` (v0.22.11) ŌĆö `gbrain storage status [--repo P] [--json]`. Split into pure data (`getStorageStatus`) + JSON formatter + human formatter (ASCII-only per D10) matching the `orphans.ts` pattern. `PageCountsByTier` and `DiskUsageByTier` are distinct nominal types so swaps fail at compile time.
- `gbrain.yml` (brain repo root, v0.22.11) ŌĆö Optional storage tiering config. Top-level `storage:` section with `db_tracked:` and `db_only:` array-valued keys. `gbrain sync` auto-manages `.gitignore` for `db_only` paths on successful sync (skips on dry-run, blocked-by-failures, submodule context, or `GBRAIN_NO_GITIGNORE=1`). `gbrain export --restore-only [--repo P] [--type T] [--slug-prefix S]` repopulates missing `db_only` files from the database.
- `src/core/supabase-admin.ts` ŌĆö Supabase admin API (project discovery, pgvector check)
- `src/core/file-resolver.ts` ŌĆö File resolution with fallback chain (local -> .redirect.yaml -> .redirect -> .supabase)
- `src/core/chunkers/` ŌĆö 3-tier chunking (recursive, semantic, LLM-guided). v0.19.0 adds `code.ts` ŌĆö tree-sitter-based semantic chunker for 29 languages with embedded-asset WASMs (`src/assets/wasm/`), `@dqbd/tiktoken` cl100k_base tokenizer, small-sibling merging. `CHUNKER_VERSION` constant folded into `importCodeFile`'s `content_hash` so chunker shape changes force clean re-chunks across releases.
- `src/core/errors.ts` (v0.19.0) ŌĆö `StructuredAgentError` + `buildError` + `serializeError`. Every new v0.19.0 agent-facing surface (code-def, code-refs, usage errors) uses this envelope; matches v0.17.0 `CycleReport.PhaseResult.error` shape.
- `src/assets/wasm/` (v0.19.0) ŌĆö 36 tree-sitter grammar WASMs + tree-sitter runtime. Committed to the repo so `bun --compile` embeds them deterministically via `import path from ... with { type: 'file' }`. The CI guard `scripts/check-wasm-embedded.sh` fails the build if the compiled binary ever silently falls through to recursive chunks.
- `src/commands/code-def.ts` + `src/commands/code-refs.ts` (v0.19.0) ŌĆö symbol definition + references lookup. Query `content_chunks.symbol_name` or chunk_text ILIKE with `page_kind='code'` filter. Auto-JSON when stdout is not a TTY (gh-CLI convention). Bypass the standard `searchKeyword` `DISTINCT ON (slug)` collapse so multiple call-sites from the same file surface.
- `src/core/search/` ŌĆö Hybrid search: vector + keyword + RRF + multi-query expansion + dedup. As of v0.22.0, `searchKeyword` / `searchKeywordChunks` / `searchVector` apply source-aware ranking at the SQL layer (curated content like `originals/`, `concepts/`, `writing/` outranks bulk content like `wintermute/chat/`, `daily/`, `media/x/`). `searchVector` uses a two-stage CTE so source-boost re-ranking doesn't kill the HNSW index. Hard-exclude prefixes (`test/`, `archive/`, `attachments/`, `.raw/` by default) filter at retrieval, not post-rank. Both gates honor `detail !== 'high'` so temporal queries surface chat pages normally.
- `src/core/search/intent.ts` ŌĆö Query intent classifier (entity/temporal/event/general ŌåÆ auto-selects detail level)
- `src/core/search/eval.ts` ŌĆö Retrieval eval harness: P@k, R@k, MRR, nDCG@k metrics + runEval() orchestrator
- `src/core/search/source-boost.ts` (v0.22.0) ŌĆö Source-type boost map keyed by slug prefix. `DEFAULT_SOURCE_BOOSTS` (originals/ 1.5, concepts/ 1.3, writing/ 1.4, people/companies/deals/ 1.2, daily/ 0.8, media/x/ 0.7, wintermute/chat/ 0.5) and `DEFAULT_HARD_EXCLUDES` (test/, archive/, attachments/, .raw/). `parseSourceBoostEnv` / `parseHardExcludesEnv` parse comma-separated `prefix:factor` pairs from `GBRAIN_SOURCE_BOOST` / `GBRAIN_SEARCH_EXCLUDE` env vars. `resolveBoostMap` and `resolveHardExcludes` merge defaults + env + caller `SearchOpts.exclude_slug_prefixes`/`include_slug_prefixes`.
- `src/core/search/sql-ranking.ts` (v0.22.0) ŌĆö Pure SQL string builders. `buildSourceFactorCase(slugColumn, boostMap, detail)` emits a CASE expression with longest-prefix-match wins (returns literal `'1.0'` when `detail === 'high'` for temporal-bypass parity with COMPILED_TRUTH_BOOST). `buildHardExcludeClause(slugColumn, prefixes)` emits `NOT (col LIKE 'p1%' OR col LIKE 'p2%')` ŌĆö OR-chain wrapped in NOT, NOT `NOT LIKE ALL/ANY` (those quantifiers don't express set-exclusion). LIKE meta-character escape covers all three of `%`, `_`, AND `\` (backslash matters because it's Postgres LIKE's default escape char). Single-quote doubling on SQL string literals so injection-style inputs are inert text.
- `src/commands/eval.ts` ŌĆö `gbrain eval` command: single-run table + A/B config comparison. v0.25.0 adds sub-subcommand dispatch on `args[0]` so `gbrain eval export` + `gbrain eval prune` + `gbrain eval replay` route into session-capture handlers; bare `gbrain eval --qrels ŌĆ”` fall-through preserves the legacy IR-metrics flow.
- `src/commands/eval-export.ts` (v0.25.0) ŌĆö streams `eval_candidates` rows as NDJSON to stdout with `schema_version: 1` prefix on every line. EPIPE-safe, progress heartbeats on stderr, stable id-desc tiebreaker so `--since` windows never dupe/miss rows.
- `src/commands/eval-prune.ts` (v0.25.0) ŌĆö explicit retention cleanup. Requires `--older-than DUR`. `--dry-run` reports would-delete count.
- `src/commands/eval-replay.ts` (v0.25.0) ŌĆö contributor-facing replay tool. Reads NDJSON from `gbrain eval export`, re-runs each captured `query` / `search` op against the current brain, computes set-Jaccard@k between captured + current `retrieved_slugs`, top-1 stability rate, and latency ╬ö. Stable JSON shape (`schema_version: 1`) for CI gating; human mode prints a regression table. Pure Bun, zero new deps. The dev-loop half of BrainBench-Real that closes the gap between "data captured" and "data used to gate a PR." See `docs/eval-bench.md` for the workflow.
- `docs/eval-bench.md` (v0.25.0) ŌĆö contributor guide for using captured data to benchmark retrieval changes before merging. Linked from CONTRIBUTING.md under "Running real-world eval benchmarks (touching retrieval code)".
- `src/core/eval-capture.ts` (v0.25.0) ŌĆö op-layer capture wrapper called from `src/core/operations.ts` `query` + `search` handlers. Catches MCP + CLI + subagent tool-bridge from one site. Fire-and-forget; failures route to `engine.logEvalCaptureFailure` so `gbrain doctor` sees drops cross-process. **Capture is off by default** ŌĆö `isEvalCaptureEnabled` resolution: explicit `config.eval.capture` (true/false) wins, else `process.env.GBRAIN_CONTRIBUTOR_MODE === '1'`, else off. Production users get a quiet brain; contributors set `export GBRAIN_CONTRIBUTOR_MODE=1` in `.zshrc` to enable the dev loop. PII scrubber gate is independent and defaults to true regardless of CONTRIBUTOR_MODE.
- `src/core/eval-capture-scrub.ts` (v0.25.0) ŌĆö zero-deps PII scrubber: emails, phones, SSN, Luhn-verified credit cards, JWT-shaped tokens, bearer tokens.

- `docs/eval-capture.md` (v0.25.0) ŌĆö stable NDJSON schema reference for gbrain-evals consumers.
- `test/public-exports.test.ts` (v0.25.0 / R2) ŌĆö runtime contract test. Imports each of the 17 public subpaths via package name and pins a canary symbol per module. Paired with `scripts/check-exports-count.sh`.
- `src/core/embedding.ts` ŌĆö OpenAI text-embedding-3-large, batch, retry, backoff
- `src/core/check-resolvable.ts` ŌĆö Resolver validation: reachability, MECE overlap, DRY checks, structured fix objects. v0.14.1: `CROSS_CUTTING_PATTERNS.conventions` is an array (notability gate accepts both `conventions/quality.md` and `_brain-filing-rules.md`). New `extractDelegationTargets()` parses `> **Convention:**`, `> **Filing rule:**`, and inline backtick references. DRY suppression is proximity-based via `DRY_PROXIMITY_LINES = 40`.
- `src/core/repo-root.ts` ŌĆö Shared `findRepoRoot(startDir?)` (v0.16.4): walks up from `startDir` (default `process.cwd()`) looking for `skills/RESOLVER.md`. Zero-dependency module imported by both `doctor.ts` and `check-resolvable.ts`. Parameterized `startDir` makes tests hermetic.

Exit rule: **1 on any issue (warnings OR errors)**, stricter than doctor's `ok` flag ŌĆö honors README:259.
